{"step_by_step_thinking": "Histoplasma capsulatum and Sporothrix globosa are both dimorphic fungi, meaning they can exist as yeast at 37°C and as hyphae at 25°C. However, the query specifically asks for an option that matches this description. Candida albicans is also dimorphic and fits this description.
